name: Aihualama Trail
tagline: The trail that starts where Manoa Falls ends — climb up into the bamboo forest most people skip.
parking: Use the Manoa Falls parking lot ($7 non-resident, free for residents) at 3739 Manoa Road
bestTime: Morning when the bamboo forest is misty and magical
kamaaina: Free parking with Hawaii state ID
talkStory: This trail follows an ancient Hawaiian footpath that once connected Manoa to Nu'uanu Valley over the ridge — the name refers to a mo'o (lizard guardian spirit) of the upper valley streams. The bamboo groves were planted during early 1900s reforestation after sandalwood harvesting stripped the hillsides bare. Locals loop it with Pauoa Flats and Nu'uanu Trail for a 6-mile circuit through the Tantalus network, leaving the hundreds of tourists crowding Manoa Falls far below.
